LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ADJUDICATION AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CLAIMS IS DENIED. THE BURDEN SHIFTED AND PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION THAT IS NOT BARRED BY D'AMICO. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL TORTS AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COUNSEL WILL MEET AND CONFER ON PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ITT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. RULING: DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY REQUESTED TO TAKE ITS MOTION OFF CALENDAR SINCE PARTIES JUST SETTLED YESTERDAY. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST AND ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOYER DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE. DEFENDANT DID NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE FOR THE RECORD IS NOT SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT THE PRODUCTS IDENTIFIED BY PLAINTIFF AND MOYER ARE THE SAME PRODUCTS. SEE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO UNDISPUTED FACTS 17, 18 AND 19. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MEERE DECLARATION IS ADMITTED. THE DECLARATION OF KELLER IS NOT FOR LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO EVENTS AT ISSUE THAT TOOK PLACE PRIOR TO 1977. THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE SHIFTED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE CONDITION OF THE SHIP AT TURNOVER. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF THE VESSEL BEING ON THE RIVER AND ON ISSUE OF TURNING THE VESSEL OVER TO THE STEVEDORE IN A REASONABLY SAFE CONDITION. THE DECLARATIONS OF AY AND COHEN ELABORATE ON THE RISK. ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS, ORDER SIGNED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Ana T. Portillo, Esq. of Jackson & Wallace LLP 982-6300 for deft Durametallic Corporation; Mary Kirk Hillyard, Esq. of Law Offices of Prindle, Decker & Amaro LLP 788-8354 for deft ITT Corporation; Denise Savoie Blocker, Esq. & Jennifer D. Fitzpatrick, Esq. of Armstrong & Associates, LLP 510/433-1830 for deft & moving party Seariver Maritime, Inc.; Deborah A. Smith, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 875-3383 for deft The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MEERE DECLARATION IS ADMITTED. THE DECLARATION OF KELLER IS NOT FOR LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO EVENTS AT ISSUE THAT TOOK PLACE PRIOR TO 1977. THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE SHIFTED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE CONDITION OF THE SHIP AT TURNOVER. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF THE VESSEL BEING ON THE RIVER AND ON ISSUE OF TURNING THE VESSEL OVER TO THE STEVEDORE IN A REASONABLY SAFE CONDITION. THE DECLARATIONS OF AY AND COHEN ELABORATE ON THE RISK. Order denying motion for summary judgment signed in open court. Court ruled on defendant's evidentiary objections, order signed. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 9956

Fitzpatrick, Esq. of Armstrong & Associates, LLP 510/433-1830 for deft Seariver Maritime, Inc.; Deborah A. Smith, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 875-3383 for deft The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company.
